# Safety Profile — [Scientific Name] ([Common Name])

> **Last reviewed:** [YYYY-MM-DD]
> **Reviewed by:** scientific-qa-evidence-agent
> **Evidence basis:** [list source types used]

---

> **Safety rule:** Safety sections are required for every BMS Archive monograph. If no data exists for a section, write `Insufficient data` — never write "safe" or leave sections blank. Absence of reported adverse events is not evidence of safety.

---

## 1. Adverse Effects

[Document all reported adverse effects. For each effect, state: severity, frequency (if known), reversibility, study type from which it was reported, and PMID/source.]

**Categorized by severity:**

| Severity | Effect | Frequency | Source type | PMID/Reference |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Mild | [e.g. GI discomfort] | [common / uncommon / rare / unknown] | [RCT / case report / etc.] | [PMID] |
| Moderate | | | | |
| Severe | | | | |

**Summary:** [Short narrative of adverse effect profile based on available evidence]

If no adverse effects are documented in the literature: "No adverse effects have been formally documented in clinical trials. However, the available evidence base is limited. Surveillance data is insufficient to rule out adverse effects."

## 2. Contraindications

[List all documented and precautionary contraindications. Distinguish between evidence-based contraindications and precautionary ones.]

**Documented contraindications (evidence-based):**
- [Condition or medication — Evidence: clinical trial / case report / pharmacological basis]

**Precautionary contraindications (no direct human evidence, but mechanistically plausible):**
- [Condition or medication — Rationale: [pharmacological mechanism]]

**Standard precautions:**
- Hypersensitivity to any component of the preparation
- [Any species-specific allergy cross-reactivity]
- Pre-surgery: [recommend discontinuation N weeks before surgery if platelet/coagulation effects are known]

---

## 3. Drug Interactions

[Document all known and potential herb–drug interactions. Rate severity using the scale below. Flag even theoretical interactions based on pharmacology.]

**Interaction severity scale:**
- **Contraindicated:** Use together is strongly discouraged; serious harm may result
- **Major:** High risk of serious adverse event; use together only if benefits clearly outweigh risks
- **Moderate:** Interaction may cause significant adverse effect; monitor closely
- **Minor:** Interaction unlikely to cause significant harm; monitor for unexpected effects
- **Theoretical:** Based on pharmacology, not confirmed by clinical evidence; flag for monitoring

| Drug / Drug class | Mechanism | Severity | Evidence | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Warfarin / anticoagulants | [e.g. additive antiplatelet effect / CYP2C9 interaction] | [severity] | [clinical / case / in vitro] | [Monitor INR / Avoid] |
| CYP3A4 substrates | [inhibition / induction] | [severity] | [in vitro / clinical] | [Monitor] |
| [Drug class] | | | | |

**CYP450 and transporter interactions:**
- CYP3A4: [inhibitor / inducer / substrate / No known interaction / Unknown]
- CYP2C9: [specify]
- CYP1A2: [specify]
- P-glycoprotein: [specify]

**Note:** In vitro CYP450 interactions may or may not be clinically relevant. Clinical significance depends on dose, preparation, and individual variation.

---

## 4. Pregnancy

[Address use during pregnancy. State clearly: contraindicated / use with caution / insufficient data / not recommended.]

**Recommendation:** [Contraindicated / Not recommended / Insufficient data — avoid / May be used under medical supervision]

**Evidence basis:**
- Human clinical data: [exists / not available]
- Animal reproductive toxicity data: [findings or "not available"]
- Traditional use during pregnancy: [documented / not documented]
- Regulatory guidance: [EMA/WHO position if available]

**Detail:** [Specific concerns: uterotonic effects, teratogenicity, fetal developmental effects, placental transfer. If no data: "No adequate human or animal reproductive toxicity studies are available. Use during pregnancy is not recommended due to insufficient safety data."]

---

## 5. Lactation

[Address use during breastfeeding.]

**Recommendation:** [Contraindicated / Not recommended / Insufficient data — avoid / May be used under medical supervision]

**Evidence basis:**
- Excretion in breast milk: [documented / not studied / unknown]
- Effects on nursing infant: [known risks / unknown]

**Detail:** [If no data: "It is unknown whether [compound] or its metabolites are excreted in human breast milk. As a precautionary measure, use during lactation is not recommended."]

---

## 6. Hepatic Impairment

[Address use in patients with liver disease.]

**Recommendation:** [Contraindicated / Use with caution / Standard dosing / Insufficient data]

**Evidence:**
- Hepatotoxicity reports: [yes, case reports: [N] / no / unknown]
- Effect of hepatic impairment on metabolism: [known / unknown]
- Dose adjustment needed: [yes / no / unknown]

**Detail:** [If hepatotoxicity cases exist, describe them. If unknown: "No pharmacokinetic studies in patients with hepatic impairment are available. Caution is advised given that [compound] is metabolized hepatically."]

---

## 7. Renal Impairment

[Address use in patients with kidney disease.]

**Recommendation:** [Contraindicated / Use with caution / Standard dosing / Insufficient data]

**Evidence:**
- Renal excretion of active compounds: [yes — specify % / no / unknown]
- Dose accumulation risk: [yes / no / unknown]
- Dose adjustment needed: [yes / no / unknown]

**Detail:** [If unknown: "No pharmacokinetic data in patients with renal impairment is available. Caution is advised if active compounds are renally excreted."]

## 8. Pediatric Use

**Recommendation:** [Not recommended / Insufficient data / Avoid]

**Age threshold (if applicable):** [minimum age from regulatory guidance or studies]

**Considerations:**
- Polypharmacy risk: [high / moderate / low — based on drug interaction profile]
- Pharmacokinetic changes in elderly: [relevant / not established]
- Falls / sedation / cognitive risk: [if applicable]

## 14. Evidence Limitations

[Summarize limitations of the safety evidence base.]

- [ ] Limited number of subjects studied
- [ ] Short trial duration (few studies exceed [N] weeks)
- [ ] Lack of standardized preparations — results may not generalize
- [ ] Reporting bias — adverse events may be underreported
- [ ] No long-term safety data available
- [ ] Population-specific data (elderly, pediatric, pregnant) absent
- [ ] Drug interaction studies are largely theoretical (in vitro only)
- [Additional limitations specific to this species]

## Disclaimer

This safety profile is for informational and research purposes only. It does not constitute medical advice. Clinical decisions regarding the use of this compound should be made in consultation with a qualified healthcare provider. BMS Archive holds no liability for clinical decisions based on this information.
